what: Cost of one Claude agent-to-agent coordination round-trip — the minimal possible subagent (reply 'pong', zero tools). Establishes the per-coordination 'communication tax' Claude pays that an Agix bus signal avoids.
method: Each sample = one Agent-tool subagent dispatched from the main session; usage object read directly (subagent_tokens, duration_ms). HAND-RECORDED from the harness, not a CI-runnable probe (it requires the Agent runtime). N=16 across two batches (4 sequential + 12 concurrent).
reproducibility: Re-run by dispatching N minimal subagents and recording the usage object. Tokens are deterministic to ~5 tokens; duration depends on load + concurrency.

tokens.note: essentially CONSTANT — fixed per-spawn overhead (system prompt + tool defs), invariant to the trivial task → it is overhead, not work

duration_ms.note: mean inflated by one concurrency-scheduling outlier (8647ms); median/trimmed-mean are the honest central tendency

honest_scope: This is the cost of a COORDINATION round-trip (a handoff/signal), not of reasoning. A bus avoids it only for coordination steps that need no fresh reasoning; reasoning steps cost an LLM call in either architecture. See wiki/research/EFFICIENCY_BENCHMARK_COMMUNICATION_TAX_2026-06-18.md.
